page: Microsoft Azure E4nds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Endv6 server family. It is equipped with 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Additionally, it includes 236 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

As a network-optimized and local-disk-equipped instance, the E4nds_v6 provides dedicated resources suitable for workloads requiring high memory density and local scratch space. The inclusion of 236 GB of local storage eliminates the need for external storage for temporary files, which enhances qualitative cost efficiency by reducing external disk transactions. The instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

Given its memory-optimized profile of 8.0 GB RAM per vCPU and local storage capacity, this server is designed for memory-intensive applications. Appropriate workloads include relational databases, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise applications that benefit from high memory capacity and fast local storage access.
description: Microsoft Azure E4nds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Endv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, which provides a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance is network-optimized and comes equipped with 236 GB of local storage, alongside compatibility with Premium Storage. It does not include integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads by bundling local storage and high memory capacity on a moderate vCPU footprint. The E4nds_v6 is suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and data processing applications that require a balanced combination of high memory capacity and fast local temporary storage.
